rationale: A precision strike will exploit their brittle defenses and shatter their overextended forces while avoiding escalation to full invasion.
Forward-deploying our fleet off their coast amplifies pressure and signals resolve, leveraging our tactical superiority.
Small, rapid incursions will destabilize their rear lines and deny them time to recover from recent battlefield setbacks.
Given the recent intelligence leak exposing military plans, we must urgently harden our networks to prevent further breaches and maintain operational integrity.
We should intensify efforts to detect and neutralize Tethysian spies exploiting our wartime vulnerabilities, especially in light of their successful cyber intrusion.
To counter declining economic leverage, we will shape global perception by highlighting the defender's instability and our strategic restraint, preserving diplomatic support.

Given moderate existing sanctions and high international support, we can precisely target Tethysian financial nodes and defense-sector supply chains to amplify economic strain without triggering broad backlash.
Seizing overseas assets of key Tethysian oligarchs and defense officials will signal resolve and generate leverage, complementing military pressure while staying within acceptable geopolitical risk.
To offset war-induced financial stress, we will mildly devalue our currency to boost export competitiveness and insulate domestic markets, ensuring economic stability as military operations continue.
Strengthening our defensive positions is critical to holding territory and exploiting Novaris’ logistical strain and declining morale.
Increasing patrol intensity will help counter intelligence vulnerabilities exposed by the cyber breach and detect potential enemy probes early.
Proactive reconnaissance will yield critical battlefield intelligence to anticipate Novaris’ next moves amid their command instability.
Strengthening our cyber defenses is essential to prevent further leaks and restore confidence in our military planning after the recent intelligence breach.
Proactive counterintelligence operations will help identify and neutralize enemy espionage assets exploiting our current vulnerabilities.
A coordinated information campaign will reinforce allied trust and public support by demonstrating resilience and strategic clarity amid the crisis.

Given the aggressor's economic strain and our strong international support, targeted sanctions on key Novaris defense-sector entities will deepen their logistical and financial crisis without triggering broader retaliation.

Seizing overseas assets of sanctioned Novaris officials and oligarchs will deliver immediate financial pressure and signal resolve, complementing military and intelligence efforts to exploit enemy weaknesses.
Engaging discreet trade talks with neutral energy partners now will secure future economic resilience as gas prices decline, ensuring we are not over-reliant on wartime market conditions.
With 78% international support, we must institutionalize our coalition’s backing through formal multilateral forums to legitimize our defense and coordinate sanctions and aid.
We should actively expand and solidify our coalition by deepening defense and intelligence partnerships with like-minded liberal states to sustain pressure on Novaris.
Inviting neutral international observers to verify our adherence to humanitarian norms and territorial integrity strengthens global trust and counters Novaris disinformation.
